subject: consolidate Policy Health Check into PAP
commit_message:
consolidate Policy Health Check into PAP

Issue-ID: POLICY-1689
Change-Id: I3b84094e217c8cda115efdbf3444e92f08013a7c
Signed-off-by: Hengye <yehui.wang@est.tech>

 > "pdpState": "ACTIVE",
 > "healthy": "HEALTHY"
 > }
 > ],
 > "drools": [
 > {
 > "instanceId": "CN-00000994",
 > "pdpState": "ACTIVE",
 > "healthy": "HEALTHY"
 > }
 > ],
 > "apex": [
 > {
 > "instanceId": "apex_57",
 > "pdpState": "ACTIVE",
 > "healthy": "HEALTHY",
 > "message": "Pdp Heartbeat"
 > }
 > ]
 > },
 > "Policy Distribution": {
 > "name": "Policy SSD",
 > "url": "http://CN-00000994:6970/healthcheck",
 > "healthy": true,
 > "code": 200,
 > "message": "alive"
 > },
 > "Policy PAP": {
 > "name": "Policy PAP",
 > "url": "http://CN-00000994:6967/policy/pap/v1/healthcheck",
 > "healthy": true,
 > "code": 200,
 > "message": "alive"
 > },
 > "Policy API": {
 > "name": "Policy API",
 > "url": "http://CN-00000994:6966/policy/api/v1/healthcheck",
 > "healthy": true,
 > "code": 200,
 > "message": "alive"
 > },
 > "Health Status": true
 > }

The URL's above needs change. And should be https://<container-host-name>:6969/<URI Path>

Okay, as you suggested, I group pdps with types, pdps with the same type no matter from different groups would be organized together :

{
    "Policy PDPs": {
        "xacml": [
            {
                "instanceId": "CN-00000994",
                "pdpState": "ACTIVE",
                "healthy": "HEALTHY"
            }
        ],
        "drools": [
            {
                "instanceId": "CN-00000994",
                "pdpState": "ACTIVE",
                "healthy": "HEALTHY"
            }
        ],
        "apex": [
            {
                "instanceId": "CN-00000994",
                "pdpState": "ACTIVE",
                "healthy": "HEALTHY",
                "message": "Pdp Heartbeat"
            }
        ]
    },
    "Policy Distribution": {
        "name": "Policy SSD",
        "url": "http://policy-distribution:6969/healthcheck",
        "healthy": true,
        "code": 200,
        "message": "alive"
    },
    "Policy PAP": {
        "name": "Policy PAP",
        "url": "http://policy-pap:6969/policy/pap/v1/healthcheck",
        "healthy": true,
        "code": 200,
        "message": "alive"
    },
    "Policy API": {
        "name": "Policy API",
        "url": "http://policy-api:6969/policy/api/v1/healthcheck",
        "healthy": true,
        "code": 200,
        "message": "alive"
    },
    "Health Status": true
}

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-pap-master-verify-java/395

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Thanks for the work.

I think we should follow the same naming convention for json fields uniformly.   The mostly used standard in place for our messages, is camel case starting with lower case no space separating words, the white spacing, is new, I don't think we have any messaging as such.   Note that whitespaces makes life more difficult to tooling.

Under that trend of thought, I suggest changing fields as "Health Status" to a single word: "healthy" which is consistent with the inner report.   This is also what the robot framework currently checked in previous releases.   Along the same lines, "Policy Distribution" should change to something like "distribution", camel case no whitespaces.   "Policy" can be dropped as it is obvious from the URL that is talking to the policy subsystem.

There is additional future work that would be needed to consider:

1. Go under the robot source code and change existing healthcheck calls to use this API.   We should open a ticket to track this work.

2. Consider work to also healthchecking legacy components such as legacy pap , pdp-x, nexus, and mariadb.    We need an strategy on how to do it.   This is future work.
